_comment: D-09 decode-half fixtures. Each scenario drives a Blend pool Config contract_data change through DecodeState and asserts the resulting LedgerState liveness. These pin the CAP-0062/Protocol-23 trap: eviction is a Live=false signal and TTL-expiry is LiveUntilLedgerSeq < ledgerSeq, both distinct from a value being present. Close-meta-level extraction (EvictedLedgerKeys / TtlEntry fold) is plan V's extract_test.go; here we only assert the decode consumes the signals.

scenarios.description: Live=false (the relay extract sets this from EvictedLedgerKeys) removes a previously-live pool from LedgerState.
A change whose LiveUntilLedgerSeq is below ledgerSeq is expired, so the pool is not live even though Live=true and a value is present.
ChangeType=Restored with Live=true and a fresh LiveUntilLedgerSeq re-livens a pool that had been evicted (no prior state).
